body:
This PR fixes four documentation inconsistencies found by reviewing the `documentation/` folder against the current source code and repository structure.

## Changes

### `using-azure-identity.md` — outdated `DefaultAzureCredential` credential chain
The `DefaultAzureCredential` section listed only `EnvironmentCredential` and `ManagedIdentityCredential` in its chain. The actual implementation in `sdk/identity/identity/src/credentials/defaultAzureCredential.ts` tries **eight** credentials in order. Added the missing six:
- `WorkloadIdentityCredential`
- `VisualStudioCodeCredential`
- `AzureCliCredential`
- `AzurePowerShellCredential`
- `AzureDeveloperCliCredential`
- `BrokerCredential` (with a note that it requires `@azure/identity-broker`)

### `writing-performance-tests.md` — non-existent `tsconfig.package` reference
The example `tsconfig.json` for a perf test project used `"extends": "../../../../tsconfig.package"`. No `tsconfig.package.json` file exists at the repo root. The actual template perf test (`sdk/template/template-perf-tests/tsconfig.json`) extends `tsconfig.lib.json`, so the example was updated to `"extends": "../../../../tsconfig.lib.json"`.

### `resolve-pnpm-lock-merge-conflict.md` — missing `/` in `git add` one-liner
The combined shell command that resolves the merge conflict in one step had a typo: the `git add` part was missing the `/` path separator between the shell-substituted repo root and `pnpm-lock.yaml`:
```shell
# Before (broken)
git add `git rev-parse --show-toplevel`pnpm-lock.yaml
# After (fixed)
git add `git rev-parse --show-toplevel`/pnpm-lock.yaml
```

### `steps-after-generations.md` — missing `...` suffix on three `pnpm build --filter` commands
Three code blocks (Windows record, Linux playback, Windows playback) used `pnpm build --filter=${PACKAGE_NAME}` without the trailing `...`. Per repo conventions (and `CONTRIBUTING.md`), the `...` suffix is required to also build dependencies. The Linux record block already had `...`; the other three were inconsistent and have been fixed.
title: docs: fix inconsistencies in documentation folder
